children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Action plans shall be implemented, insofar as practicable, to upgrade existing industries to use natural resources more efficiently and to expand the economic base of rural communities so as to alleviate or reduce their dependence on National Forest System land resources.

children.heading: Assistance
children.label: b Assistance
children.text: To implement action plans, the Secretary may make grants and enter into cooperative agreements and contracts to provide necessary technical and related assistance. Such grants, cooperative agreements, and contracts may be with the affected rural community, State and local governments, universities, corporations, and other persons.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: c Limitation
children.text: The Federal contribution to the overall implementation of an action plan shall not exceed 80 percent of the total cost of the plan, including administrative and other costs. In calculating the Federal contribution, the Secretary shall take into account the fair market value of equipment, personnel, and services provided.

children.heading: Available authority
children.label: d Available authority
children.text: The Secretary may use the Secretary’s authority under the Cooperative Forestry Assistance Act of 1978 ( et seq.) and other Federal, State, and local governmental authorities in implementing action plans.16 U.S.C. 2101

children.heading: Consistency with forest plans
children.label: e Consistency with forest plans
children.text: The implementation of action plans shall be consistent with land and resource management plans.
